task: Write a Python function balanced(s) returning True when the parentheses in the string are balanced, considering only the characters '(' and ')' and ignoring everything else. A closing parenthesis must never appear before its opening partner: ')(' is not balanced. An empty string is balanced. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code effectively handles an empty string as a balanced input.
trace: I used a Stack data structure to keep track of opening parentheses encountered in the string. Whenever a closing parenthesis is found, it checks if there is an opening parenthesis on top of the stack or if the stack is empty. If either condition is true, it returns False. The function returns True only when the stack is empty at the end, meaning all parentheses have been matched properly. I handled an edge case where an unexpected closing parenthesis appears before its corresponding opening parenthesis.
